films.name: Kodak Gold 200
films.notes: Consumer general-purpose film; warm tones, good latitude.

films.name: Kodak UltraMax 400
films.notes: Affordable, versatile consumer film; good for everyday and travel.

films.name: Kodak Portra 160
films.notes: Fine grain, natural skin tones; ideal for portraits in good light.

films.name: Kodak Portra 400
films.notes: The industry standard portrait film; excellent latitude, beautiful skin tones.

films.name: Kodak Portra 800
films.notes: High-speed portrait film; retains warmth and skin tones in low light.

films.name: Kodak Ektar 100
films.notes: World's finest-grain color negative; ultra-saturated; ideal for landscapes.

films.name: Kodak Pro Image 100
films.notes: Budget-friendly professional film; neutral tones; good for portraits outdoors.

films.name: Fujifilm 200
films.notes: Entry-level consumer film; cool/neutral tones.

films.name: Fujifilm 400
films.notes: Versatile consumer film; cool/green tones; budget-friendly.

films.name: CineStill 50D
films.notes: Daylight-balanced cinema stock (Vision3 50D base); extremely fine grain; C-41 processable.

films.name: CineStill 400D
films.notes: Daylight-balanced cinema stock; vibrant, contrasty rendering; C-41 processable.

films.name: CineStill 800T
films.notes: Tungsten-balanced cinema stock; warm glow/halation at highlights; adjust white balance for daylight use. Best for night and artificial-light scenes.

films.name: Ilford HP5 Plus
films.notes: Iconic general-purpose B&W film; great pushed to 800, 1600, or 3200.

films.name: Ilford FP4 Plus
films.notes: Fine-grain medium-speed B&W; excellent tonal range; ideal for landscapes.

films.name: Ilford Delta 100
films.notes: T-grain B&W; ultra-fine grain; excellent for studio and landscape work.

films.name: Ilford Delta 400
films.notes: T-grain B&W; sharp and fine for 400 speed; pushes well to 800.

films.name: Ilford Delta 3200
films.notes: Native ISO ~1000; marketed at 3200; rate at 1600–3200 and push process. Pronounced grain adds atmosphere.

films.name: Kodak Tri-X 400
films.notes: Legendary grain and contrast; pushes to 1600–3200 beautifully.

films.name: Kodak T-Max 100
films.notes: T-grain film; extremely fine grain; excellent sharpness for landscapes and studio.

films.name: Kodak T-Max 400
films.notes: T-grain; finest grain of any 400-speed B&W; pushes to 3200.

films.name: Kodak T-Max P3200
films.notes: Native ISO ~800–1000; suggest exposing at 1600–3200 with push processing. Classic photojournalism film.

films.name: Kodak Ektachrome E100
films.notes: Revived transparency film; cool/neutral tones; vivid colors; strict metering required.

films.name: Fujifilm Velvia 50
films.notes: Extremely saturated and contrasty slide film; strict metering required; iconic for nature and landscape.

films.name: Fujifilm Velvia 100
films.notes: Slightly less saturated than Velvia 50; more flexible for varied lighting.

films.name: Fujifilm Provia 100F
films.notes: Natural, balanced transparency film; fine grain; versatile for portraits and landscapes.

films.name: Kentmere 400
films.notes: Budget-friendly B&W film; good latitude; slightly more grain than Delta/T-Max at 400.
